subject: Joint Distributions
topic: §42 Sum of Two Independent Random Variables: Continuous Case
question: Let $X$ be an exponential random variable with parameter $\lambda$ and $Y$ be a uniform random variable on $[0, 1]$ independent of $X$. Find the probability density function of $X + Y$.
answer: $f_{X+Y}(a) = \begin{cases} 1 - e^{-\lambda a} & 0 \le a \le 1 \\ e^{-\lambda a}(e^{\lambda} - 1) & a \ge 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$
explanation: Answer: $f_{X+Y}(a) = \begin{cases} 1 - e^{-\lambda a} & 0 \le a \le 1 \\ e^{-\lambda a}(e^{\lambda} - 1) & a \ge 1 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$
